dissent_logged: Technical seat: the drip mechanism already bought the 334 dip and the setup targets 400 — redirecting forfeits the next leg. Fundamental edge is real: PEG 1.39 is the cheapest mega-cap AI name and the FCF yield compression is discretionary-capex driven, not structural erosion. These are valid points but do not override the allocation-structure argument.
cio_memo: GOOG is a satellite position, not a v3 core sleeve. The $500/wk drip belongs in RSP (primary v3 core) or VXUS (international) until one of two conditions is met. The dual-book incoherence is the deciding factor: continuing to add $26k/yr in Fidelity while the IBKR desk is staging a 20-25% trim at $380-400 produces a net-flat or net-growing combined GOOG exposure — directly contradicting the stated trim thesis from the same advisory context. The Berkshire add (executed ~$270-310 in Q1, 45-day lag) does not endorse $358; Druckenmiller's full exit is the more recent and contemporaneous signal. Dow inclusion is an institutionalization marker, not a catalyst — the Intel-Dow 1999 analog is a tail risk, not a base case, but it illustrates that index inclusion can mark peak consensus rather than an entry point. Redirect the $500/wk to RSP until the IBKR trim resolves or GOOG reaches the $310-320 margin-of-safety zone.
circle_of_competence: Alphabet earns ~77% of revenue from advertising (Google Search + YouTube + Network), capturing high-intent commercial queries at the exact moment of purchase decision — the moat is a self-reinforcing flywheel: query volume drives relevance, relevance drives advertiser ROI, ROI drives CPCs, CPCs fund infrastructure, infrastructure improves relevance. GCP (~13% of revenue, ~28% YoY growth) adds a second moat via AI-workload lock-in. Competence confirmed.
skeptic_response: ACCEPT the dual-book incoherence argument — it is not rebutted. The IBKR book review (same advisory desk, prior session) recommends trimming GOOG 20-25% at $380-400. The Fidelity drip buys $500/wk at ~$357. The two actions are not separated by time horizon or tax structure in any documented way; both are in the same advisory umbrella. Net combined effect: GOOG exposure flat-to-growing while the stated tactical posture is reduction. This is a structural contradiction that overrides the fundamental PEG edge. The FCF-yield-under-AI-capex rebuttal is analytically valid but does not resolve which book should hold the position. The resolution is simple: redirect the Fidelity drip to v3 core (RSP/VXUS); if IBKR trim executes and reduces net GOOG, a Fidelity drip resumption becomes coherent again.
conditions_to_resume_or_change: RESUME_DRIP: IBKR trim executes (20-25% reduction of IBKR GOOG position at $380-400), netting down combined cross-book exposure — drip can then accumulate without contradicting the trim thesis.
RESUME_DRIP: GOOG corrects to $310-320 (fundamental margin-of-safety zone per DCF fair value $340-370) — new entry is value-accretive and the thesis resets.
INCREASE_REDIRECT: If AdX divestiture ruling arrives adverse or capex-ROI disappointment materializes (forward P/E compresses toward 19x), redirect portion may increase to $750/wk RSP.
INVALIDATION_CHECK: If combined GOOG across both books exceeds 5% of total portfolio, drip is imprudent regardless of thesis — enforce concentration cap first.
